prompt: Design and implement a Java caching system with multiple eviction strategies, configurable max size, thread-safe access, and cache statistics.
assertions: Cache is generic with type parameters <K, V> (Items 29-30)
Eviction strategy is behind an interface, allowing new strategies without modifying Cache (OCP)
Separate strategy implementations for LRU, LFU, and TTL (SRP)
Uses a builder pattern for cache configuration (Item 2)
CacheStats is an immutable value object (Item 17)
Thread safety is achieved via ConcurrentHashMap or explicit synchronization (Items 78-81)
Cache interface is focused — no unrelated methods bundled in (ISP)
Returns Optional for get() operations rather than null (Item 55)
